title: LM_NOTIFICATION_POSTMESSAGE
text: LM_NOTIFICATION_POSTMESSAGE If specified, this option suppresses the default message and sends an asynchronous message to the Uniface application, enabling it to provide customized handling of the expiration notice. LM_NOTIFICATION_POSTMESSAGE { =T | true | Y | yes | 1 } The message that is sent has the following properties: $msgsrc: "UNIFACE" $msgid: "LICENSING" $msgdata: UnifaceList The UnifaceList contains the following items: LICMGR —license manager used: Sentinel . FEATURE —license feature abbreviation, for example UDE . REASON —reason for the message: license , lease , version , grace , maintenance , no_more_maintenance , or commuter . DAYSLEFT —the number of days that the feature is still valid ( license ), the number of days a grace license is defined to be valid ( grace ), or the number of days the maintenance contract is still valid ( maintenance and no_more_maintenance ). In the case of no_more_maintenace , this number is negative. HOURSLEFT —number of hours left a grace license is still valid. Note: In the case of a commuter license, when a commuter license is about to expire, two messages are send. One with REASON 'license' and another with REASON 'commuter' . Only one of these needs to be handled. For example, if you handle the message with REASON 'license' , you do not need to handle the message with reason 'commuter' . Was this topic helpful?
